items.title: E-commerce churn model with 400 engineered columns
items.scenario: A team engineers 400 behavioral features (recency, frequency, category dummies). Training a gradient-boosted model on all columns is slow; product wants a documented feature list for compliance.
items.explanation: Stage 1: `VarianceThreshold` and MI filter inside a `Pipeline` keep the top 80 features on training folds only. Stage 2: `RFECV` with a lightweight logistic base on those 80 picks the final 25. The serialized pipeline ensures batch scoring and the API use the same 25 columns.

items.title: Fraud detection with daily retraining
items.scenario: A fraud team retrains nightly on rolling 30-day windows. Yesterday's selected features must match today's online scorer without manual column lists.
items.explanation: Training saves `joblib.dump(pipeline)` where the pipeline ends with `SelectFromModel(RandomForestClassifier)` fit on the nightly window. Inference loads the artifact and calls `predict`—no separate 'feature list' maintained by hand. If selection drifts night-to-night, alerts fire on support-mask changes.

items.title: Leakage from global MI ranking
items.scenario: A data scientist runs `SelectKBest(mutual_info_classif, k=50).fit_transform(X_all, y_all)` then splits 80/20 for evaluation and reports 94% accuracy.
items.explanation: Test labels influenced which 50 features were kept, so validation is optimistically biased. Fix: split first, then `Pipeline([('sel', SelectKBest(...)), ('clf', ...)])` with `cross_val_score` on train only; hold out a untouched test set for final reporting.
